mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is a comprehensive platform for deploying and managing containerized applications. It provides a robust set of tools for developers and operators, including automated installation, scaling, and monitoring. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to simpler Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift command-line interface (CLI), which is essential for interacting with your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz

tar -xzf openshift-client-linux-4.17.0.tar.gz

sudo mv oc kubectl /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the latest OpenShift CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the binaries to your PATH

steps.title: Create an OpenShift cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new OpenShift cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=mycluster --log-level=info

```

> Replace 'mycluster' with your desired directory name

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to access the newly created OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=mycluster/auth/kubeconfig

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up monitoring and autoscaling for your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/cluster-monitoring-config.yaml

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-autoscaler/master/deploy/cluster-autoscaler-config.yaml

```

> Apply the monitoring configuration
> Apply the autoscaling configuration

uninstall.title: Remove the OpenShift cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will delete the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=mycluster --log-level=info

```

> Ensure you have backups of any important data

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are removed.

```bash

kubectl delete pvc --all

kubectl delete service --all

```

> Delete all persistent volume claims
> Delete all services

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenShift cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster and drain nodes if necessary. This step upgrades the OpenShift cluster to the specified version.

```bash

oc adm upgrade --to-image=<new-version-image>

oc get nodes

```

> Replace <new-version-image> with the desired image
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade

upgrade.title: Backup cluster
upgrade.description:
Create a backup of your current cluster state before proceeding with the upgrade.

```bash

oc adm backup

```

> Backup your cluster state

upgrade.title: Drain nodes
upgrade.description:
Drain nodes to safely upgrade without disrupting running applications.

```bash

oc adm drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to drain

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OpenShift API server is not running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the OpenShift API server and ensure it is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified resource does not exist in the current namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are in the correct namespace or that the resource exists.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image cannot be pulled from the registry.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the image exists in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources to schedule the pod.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or reduce resource requests for the pod.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced security features, and improved monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs and features related to networking.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the deprecation notices and update any configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'oc get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
